text.title: retention-strategies Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for retention-strategies as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Implementing variable rewards with a non-seeded random number generator in server code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Non-reproducible reward sequences make bug reports impossible to reproduce and A/B test results impossible to verify. Require deterministic seeding for testability.

failure-mode.description: Studying only current cohorts when diagnosing retention problems.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Current cohorts are the surviving population — they exclude the signals that drove churned users out. Cohort-level analysis must include churned users to produce accurate loop diagnoses.

failure-mode.description: No clear external or internal trigger for return.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users forget products that create no habit signal. Both push/email (external) and habit/time-of-day (internal) triggers must exist; relying only on external triggers creates notification dependence that collapses when users mute the channel.
